summary: Iteration 17: Tested Designation-based Hirings module end-to-end. Backend pytest 16/17 passed (1 BUG: same-name designations across office_types fails with 500 due to legacy Mongo unique index). Frontend UI fully verified — sidebar Hirings/Jobs swap, /jobs redirect, Hirings tabs+stage counters, candidates page, Designations office_type select+badges, both Add Lead forms (HO+Franchise) carry designation* / min / max / description and the old Job dropdown is gone.

critical.issue: Creating a 2nd designation with same name but different office_type returns 500 (pymongo DuplicateKeyError on legacy index name_lower_1). App-level check uses compound (name_lower, office_type) but DB still enforces single-field unique index on name_lower. Spec requires same name to be allowed across office_types.

issues: Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' — accessibility, non-blocking

action_items: Drop legacy Mongo unique index `name_lower_1` on db.designations and create a compound unique index on (name_lower, office_type), OR remove the unique index altogether and rely on the app-level duplicate check already in routes/designations.py. Suggested one-liner: `await db.designations.drop_index('name_lower_1')` followed by `await db.designations.create_index([('name_lower',1),('office_type',1)], unique=True)` in seed_default_designations() (idempotent via try/except IndexNotFound).
Add aria-describedby / DialogDescription to Designation and Add Lead modal DialogContent to clear a11y warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/designations.py app-level dedupe is correct (name_lower + office_type) but the underlying Mongo index isn't aligned with the new model — schema migration step missing.
routes/hirings.py: stages list is hard-coded with `three_months` deliberately excluded; STAGE_DISPLAY_MAP correctly merges three_months->joined. Good.
routes/leads.py: update_lead unset of empty-string `designation_id` works; auto-fill of is_technician on update is consistent with create. Good.
Layout.js: Hirings link visible only to CEO/HR/Manager — matches spec; Jobs link fully removed from getNavItems.
test_credentials: admin@servall.com / Admin@123 (works — note: /app/memory/test_credentials.md says default seeded is ServallAdmin@123 but admin user was reset to Admin@123)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed designations and leads and clean up via /api/leads/{id}/delete + DELETE /api/designations/{id} at end (test_zz_cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration17_hirings.py after the legacy Mongo index is dropped — only the failing test `test_create_designation_dual_office_type_same_name` needs to flip to green. UI is fully working; no frontend rework needed. Other tests (filter by office_type, hirings dashboard shape, stage roll-up incl. three_months->joined merge, legacy job_role match, candidate listing) all already pass.
rca of the issue: Backend log shows pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError: E11000 dup key on index `name_lower_1` (collection servall_hiring.designations). The collection still has the OLD single-field unique index from before the office_type schema change. The POST handler in routes/designations.py:75 only checks app-level uniqueness against (name_lower, office_type) and falls through to insert_one, which then hits the stale DB index. Reproduction: POST /api/designations with name='Foo' office_type='head_office' (succeeds), then POST same name with office_type='franchise' → 500. Mitigation: drop the index and add a compound unique index (or no DB-level unique) — app already enforces correct duplicate rule.
